steps.title: Fastest path: track what changes
steps.content: When the same amount is added to every value, the mean and median move by that amount. The range and standard deviation stay the same because the spacing between values does not change.

steps.title: Use the constant-shift rule
steps.content: Data set B is created by adding $56$ to every value in data set A. Adding a constant shifts the whole dot plot right but does not stretch it out.

steps.title: Compare medians
steps.content: Data set A has $15$ values, so its median is the $8$th value. The dot plot shows that value is $23$. After adding $56$ to every value, the median of B is $23+56=79$, which is greater than the median of A.

steps.title: Compare ranges
steps.content: The minimum and maximum of data set A are $22$ and $26$, so its range is $26-22=4$. In data set B, those become $78$ and $82$, so the range is $82-78=4$. The range stays equal.
